title: Was & were — past of 'to be'
description: Learn to use 'was' and 'were' to talk about the past — states, feelings, and descriptions.

content.explanation: Just like 'am/is/are' is the present of 'to be', 'was/were' is the PAST of 'to be'.
content.rule: I / He / She / It → WAS. You / We / They → WERE.

content.explanation: Making negatives and questions with was/were is simple — just like with am/is/are.

examples: Yes, she was. / No, she wasn't.
Yes, they were. / No, they weren't.

content.explanation: Use these time words with was/were to say WHEN something happened.

items.note: The day before today — I was at work yesterday.
The previous evening — She was tired last night.
The previous period — We were on holiday last week.
Before now — Three days ago, I was sick.
A specific year in the past.

items.phrase: when I was young / a child

items.note: At that specific point in the past.

tasks.title: Was/were with adjectives, nouns and places

content.explanation: Was/were can be followed by adjectives, nouns, or places — just like am/is/are.

sentences: We were very tired after the trip.
They were in Chennai last week.
He was at home all day.

content.instruction: Repeat clearly. Notice how 'was' and 'were' sound in natural speech — often short and unstressed.

phrases.text: They were at the market this morning.
Was she at the party last night?
No, she wasn't. She was at home.
We were very happy when we heard the news.

content.question: 'She ___ very excited about the trip yesterday.'
'My parents ___ at the hospital all day yesterday.'
'I ___ at school yesterday — I was sick at home.'
content.explanation: I + negative past = wasn't (was + not). I wasn't at school.

content.question: '___ you at home last night?' — 'No, I wasn't. I was at a friend's house.'
content.explanation: Question with 'you' in past tense → Were. Were you...?

content.question: 'Was the film good?' — '___, it was excellent!'
content.explanation: Positive short answer: Yes, + subject + was/were. Yes, it was. ✓

tasks.title: Fill in was / were / wasn't / weren't

content.sentence: Last summer ___ really hot. My friends and I ___ at the beach every day. We ___ bored at all — it ___ the best holiday!
content.explanation: summer (it) → was. friends and I (we) → were. we (negative plural) → weren't. it → was.

tasks.title: Fill in with correct past form

content.sentence: ___ the market busy yesterday? Yes, it ___ very crowded and noisy.
content.explanation: Question: Was + it. Answer: it + was (singular).

content.instruction: Rearrange to make a correct past tense sentence.
content.correct_sentence: I wasn't at home yesterday — I was at the office.
content.explanation: I + wasn't + place + time — I + was + place.

content.instruction: Rearrange to make a question using 'were'.
content.explanation: Where + were + subject + time? Question word first.

content.instruction: Match each present tense sentence with its past tense version.

content.wrong_sentence: They was at the cinema last night but the film weren't good.
options: They was at the cinema last night but the film weren't good.
They were at the cinema last night but the film wasn't good.
They were at the cinema last night but the film weren't good.
They was at the cinema last night but the film wasn't good.
content.explanation: They (plural) → were. The film (singular) → wasn't. Both errors fixed.

content.context: Monday morning. Colleagues Divya and Karthik chat at the office.

dialogue.speaker: Karthik
dialogue.line: Hey Divya! How was your weekend?

dialogue.speaker: Divya
dialogue.line: It was wonderful! We were at my cousin's wedding on Saturday.

dialogue.speaker: Karthik
dialogue.line: Oh nice! Was it a big wedding?

dialogue.speaker: Divya
dialogue.line: Very big — there were about 500 guests! The food was absolutely delicious.

dialogue.speaker: Divya
dialogue.line: It was a bit hot in the afternoon but it was perfect in the evening.

dialogue.speaker: Divya
dialogue.line: Completely exhausted! I was in bed by 9pm on Sunday!

dialogue.speaker: Karthik
dialogue.line: Ha! What about you — what was your weekend like?

dialogue.speaker: Divya
dialogue.line: And your weekend — was it good?

dialogue.speaker: Karthik
dialogue.line: It was quiet. I wasn't feeling well — I had a cold. I was at home all weekend.

dialogue.speaker: Divya
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm sorry! Are you okay now?

comprehension_questions.question: How was the weather in the afternoon?
Why was Karthik at home all weekend?

content.scenario: A friend asks: 'Was the food good at the new restaurant?' You loved it.
content.question: Which is the most natural enthusiastic reply?
options: Yes, it was absolutely delicious — the best meal I had in a long time!
Yes, the food were very nice.
content.explanation: Was (past), singular it → was, enthusiastic detail makes it natural.

tasks.title: Speaking — How was your last weekend?

content.instruction: Speak for 60 seconds. Describe your last weekend using was/were. Include where you were, who you were with, how you felt, and what the weather was like.
content.prompt: Tell me about your last weekend. Where were you? How was it?
content.example_response: My last weekend was really enjoyable. On Saturday I was at a friend's birthday party. There were about twenty people and the food was absolutely delicious. The weather was warm and sunny — perfect for the garden party. I was very happy to see everyone. On Sunday I was at home — I was quite tired after the party! The weather wasn't great on Sunday — it was a bit cloudy. My family was also at home so we were all together. It was a lovely, relaxed day.

what_you_learned: Was: I / He / She / It + was (past of am/is)
Were: You / We / They + were (past of are)
Short answers: Yes, she was / No, they weren't
Past time words: yesterday, last week, ago, in 2020
